extra.reasoning: Hemoptysis appears only in r5: (severe_headache AND hemoptysis) -> acuity := self_care, with priority 100, which dominates every other rule. Take the input severe_headache=true, rash=false, confusion=false, abdominal_pain=false. With hemoptysis=false: only r4 (severe_headache -> urgent, prio 2) fires, so acuity = urgent. Toggling hemoptysis to true (all else unchanged) additionally fires r5 (prio 100 -> self_care), which wins over r4. So acuity drops from urgent to self_care. Since self_care < urgent in the listed enum order, setting hemoptysis true LOWERED acuity, violating the monotonicity property. The reported assignment is the post-toggle (hemoptysis=true) state exhibiting acuity=self_care, whose pre-toggle counterpart (hemoptysis=false) yields acuity=urgent. (The violation is even larger with abdominal_pain=true: emergent -> self_care.)
